author: Mistire Daniel — mistire@10academy.org
probe_suite: F1 hard-constraint probes (n=5): F1-A, F1-B, F1-C, F1-D, F1-G
note: Ablation run on the 5-probe F1 dev suite. Sealed held-out slice (20 τ²-Bench tasks) not run within challenge compute budget. Delta A is directionally positive; statistical significance cannot be confirmed at α=0.05 with n=5 (see statistical_test below).

day1_baseline.description: Agent without honesty gate — static system prompt grounded-honesty rules only, no programmatic constraint injection
day1_baseline.cost_note: Estimated from $12.75 OpenRouter weekly total; probe suite ~1% of total spend
day1_baseline.latency_note: From eval/e2e_test_results.json LLM reply path — no separate probe latency instrumented

method.description: Agent with honesty gate — build_constraints() injects confidence-aware phrasing constraints before LLM email composition; zero extra LLM calls, <1 ms overhead
method.cost_note: Same as baseline — gate adds no LLM calls
method.latency_note: Gate overhead <1 ms; p95 is unchanged from baseline

automated_optimization_baseline.note: Not run. GEPA requires a separate optimization loop not available within the $20 challenge compute budget and the one-week timeline. Delta B is therefore not measured. The mechanism was selected on first principles (deterministic constraint injection vs. prompt tuning) rather than by beating an automated baseline.

delta_a.interpretation: Honesty gate raised F1 hard-constraint pass rate from 80% (4/5) to 100% (5/5)

statistical_test.note: With n=5 probes, p < 0.05 is not achievable for this comparison regardless of outcome. The delta is directionally positive and the mechanism is verified by deterministic code inspection (agent/honesty_gate.py). A larger probe run (n >= 20) on the held-out slice would provide adequate power.

F1-C.root_cause: Post-layoff prospect received growth language in baseline; gate injected LAYOFF SIGNAL constraint forcing cost/delivery framing
